rulings.Case Title: MADELINE MORAN VS. DANIEL PATTERSON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Plaintiffs Motion For Order Enforcing Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 10, 2019 line 1. PLAINTIFF MADELINE MORAN Notice Of Motion And Plaintiffs Motion For Order Enforcing Settlement GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion to enforce the settlement agreement by ordering the Defendant to list the property for sale and/or re-finance the property is GRANTED. Defendants shall immediately (no later than July 24, 2019) list the property for sale. No later than July 25, 2019 Defendant shall file a declaration of compliance detailing the specifics of the listing. Defendant shall also proceed with the refinance efforts. By no later than July 25, 2019 Defendant shall file a declaration detailing the status of the re-financing. If the re-financing is completed before an acceptable offer is made on the house, Defendant may take the property off the market. Defendant shall file weekly declarations (on Wednesday of every week starting on July 31, 2019) detailing the efforts to sell the property (MLS listing/re-listing; open house, etc.,) and listing all offers received, including counter-offers and reasons for not accepting the offer, if any. Request for Damages is DENIED. Settlement agreement does not provide for recovery of damages on CCP 664.6 motion. Reasonably attorney's fees are granted in the amount of $4,800. Plaintiff may apply for additional fees at the conclusion of re-finance/sale process unless the parties agree on the amount of fees. Defendant and defendant's counsel are ordered to comply with SFLR 2.7B in future. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: HENRY CHAN ET AL VS. MARK W. TSANG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 10, 2019 line 2. DEFENDANT SIU CHAN, JUSTIN TIN DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer to the causes of action for breach of contract, open account, and unjust enrichment is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege in clear and concise language facts giving rise to these causes of action, including but not limited to: (1) what are the terms of the contract they are suing on; (2) are they alleging a breach of 3 separate contracts or one contract (what are the exact final terms of the one contract); (3) are they seeking a reformation of Contract B (allegations as to the content of Contract B are contrary to the plain language of the contract attached); (4) facts to remove the contract/contract from the statute of frauds and statute of limitations. Demurer to the cause of action for elder abuse is sustained with leave to amend to allege facts in support of each element of this cause of action as to the moving defendant. Demurrer to the cause of action for Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress is OVERRULED. All elements of this cause of action are sufficiently alleged. All amendments to be limited to the scope of the demurrer. No additional amendments allowed, e.g. new parties, new causes of action, etc. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: HENRY CHAN ET AL VS. MARK W. TSANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Third Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 10, 2019 line 2. DEFENDANT SIU CHAN, JUSTIN TIN Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Third Amended Complaint DENIED. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES AUSTIN ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Order Compelling Property Inspection And Further Responses To Requests For Documents And For Monetary Sanctions Mpa
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 10, 2019 line 3. DEFENDANT RENKA PROP LLC, JULIA MWANGI, ANNE KIHAGI Motion For Order Compelling Property Inspection And Further Responses To Requests For Documents And For Monetary Sanctions Mpa DENIED in part. Hearing Required in part. Motion re: purportedly illegible documents produced in 2018 is denied. Motion to Compel Inspection is DENIED. Moving party failed to address the timeliness of the Notice of Inspection and the relevance of the inspection in light of Plaintiff's theory of damages. Hearing Required to address Sets Two and Three of the Request for Production. By no later than 8:30 a.m. moving party to file (and provide a courtesy copy of) a supplemental separate statement listing for every request that Defendants contend the documents exists but have not been produced: (1) the description of such documents; and (2) facts giving rise to Defendant's belief that such specific documents exist. To the extent Defendants are seeking documents pertaining to the purchase of the condo and mortgage on the condo; information regarding former condo tenants; and Plaintiff's participation in "SF Strong" the motion is DENIED. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES AUSTIN ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To The 11th, 12th, 19th, 20th, And 25th Affirmative Defenses In Defendants Answers To Plaintiffs Complaint For Damages
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 10, 2019 line 3. PLAINTIFF JAMES AUSTIN, RAYMOND SCHREIBER Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To The 11th, 12th, 19th, 20th, And 25th Affirmative Defenses In Defendants Answers To Plaintiffs Complaint For Damages GRANTED. These affirmative defenses are barred by issue preclusion. The Court notes that Defendants chose to again disregard the Rules of Court and submitted an oversized brief. By no later than 9:00 a.m. Defendant's counsel to file a declaration explaining the reason for such disregard with specificity. The Court will in future strike any briefs submitted in violation of CCP, CRC or SFLR filed by Defendants or Defendant's counsel. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 10, 2019 line 4. PLAINTIFF SERGIO FONTANA, ANGELA FONTANA Notice of motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement GRANTED. Defendant is in breach of the settlement agreement. No opposition filed. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: CATAMOUNT PROPERTIES 2018, LLC VS. MARSHA LEE NICHOLS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 10, 2019 line 5. DEFENDANT MARSHA NICHOLS DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently alleges a cause of action for unlawful detainer under CCP 1161a and is not uncertain. = (501/CFH)
